name: JonDash official addons
description: Official add-on modules for JonDash. In JonDash, add this repo as a module source (Admin -> Modules -> Manage sources) to browse and install. Modules update independently of the JonDash base app. Channels: this manifest (main branch) = stable; the beta branch = beta. See VERSIONING.md.

modules.name: Health monitoring
modules.description: Watches your services with HTTP, TCP, ping, DNS and certificate checks, records uptime and response times, and alerts by email or webhook when something goes down.
modules.notes: Monitoring now starts with the server instead of when someone first opens the dashboard, so a restart overnight no longer leaves your services unwatched until morning. The advanced "how often to look for due checks" setting has been removed - it never controlled how often anything was actually checked.

modules.name: Module template (for developers)
modules.description: FOR DEVELOPERS — a working example to copy when building your own module. Installs to modules/template; open MODULE.md in that folder for a full guide, and AI-PROMPT.md to have an AI build one for you. Safe to install, and safe to uninstall when you're done.
modules.notes: The template depends on nothing: no helpers and no background work, so copying it gives you a module that installs on its own. Adds a done toggle (showing a follow-up migration and a permission the code actually uses), and a much fuller guide - including a paste-in AI prompt that covers building, testing and verifying a module end to end.

modules.name: Backup Manager
modules.description: Keeps folders copied to another location — a network share or an external drive — on a schedule, and tells you what it did. Can keep dated copies and tidy old ones away automatically.
modules.notes: First release. Copies folders you choose to somewhere else on a schedule — either keeping the destination up to date, or making a new dated copy each time. Dated copies can be tidied automatically on a grandfather-father-son schedule: keep a week of daily copies, a month of weekly ones and a year of monthly ones. Tidying DELETES old copies, so it is off until you turn it on for a particular backup, it never removes the most recent copy, it never touches anything of yours sitting alongside them, and you can preview exactly what would go first. Every run writes a log naming each file copied, skipped or failed on. It can email you or call a web address when a backup fails — and, if you ask it to, when nothing has succeeded for a while, which is the failure that otherwise goes unnoticed. JonDash's own secrets are never copied. One known gap while a JonDash fix lands: scheduled runs record everything in their own run log, but do not yet add entries to the Audit page — runs you start yourself do. Needs JonDash 1.5.2.

helpers.name: Scheduler
helpers.description: Runs modules' declared background work on time, starting when the server starts rather than when someone opens a page.
helpers.notes: Published ahead of the modules that need it. Requires JonDash 1.5.0 — helper support does not exist in 1.4.x, so nothing on the stable channel can use it until 1.5.0 ships.

helpers.name: Files and folders
helpers.description: Lets a module copy and archive folders to another location — a network share, an external drive — within the folders you allow. JonDash's own secrets are never copied.

provides.label: Look at files and folders in the locations you allow
Create and change files in the locations you allow
Delete files in the locations you allow

helpers.notes: Copies and archives folders to another location — a network share, an external drive — within folders you approve, and nothing else: it exposes no way to read a file's contents. You can point it at broad locations, including a whole drive or the folder JonDash itself lives in, because it finds JonDash's encryption key, database and HTTPS keys wherever they actually are and steps over them; moving them doesn't defeat it. Choosing a wide folder warns you about what else is in there rather than blocking it. Permissions are now enforced rather than merely described, so a module that asks for less than it uses is refused and told what it forgot to declare. Adds dated-snapshot backups with grandfather-father-son retention — keep a week of daily copies, a month of weekly ones and a year of monthly ones. Retention only ever removes folders it created itself, never the newest copy, and never anything of yours sitting alongside them; you can preview exactly what would go first, and every removal is listed in the log and the audit trail. Needs JonDash 1.5.2.
